summary: User asks how to derive the font family name from a font full name (e.g., 'FrutigerNext LT Regular') in order to use SKTypeface.FromFamilyName(), which returns the default typeface when given a full font name instead of the family name.

stepsToReproduce: Call SKTypeface.FromFamilyName('FrutigerNext LT Regular') with the full font name
Observe that the default typeface (not the intended font) is returned

analysis.summary: The user is confusing font full names (e.g., 'FrutigerNext LT Regular') with font family names (e.g., 'FrutigerNext LT'). SKTypeface.FromFamilyName() requires the family name component, not the full name. The family name can be found programmatically by enumerating SKFontManager.Default.FontFamilies, then using SKFontStyleSet.GetStyleName() to match the style portion.
analysis.rationale: This is a usage question about Skia's font model. No broken behavior exists — Skia correctly uses a family+style model, not full font names. The contributor comment in the thread already partially answered it. The required API exists (SKFontManager, SKFontStyleSet) and is publicly accessible.

keySignals.text: Skia sharp is returning default font
keySignals.interpretation: User is passing the full font name instead of the family name, causing SKTypeface to fall back to the default typeface.

keySignals.text: how can i get the correct font family name correctly for different fonts
keySignals.interpretation: Core question: how to derive the family name component from a full font name string.

keySignals.text: I cannot use load by stream in my case, where i get input only as font name
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter only has font name strings available, not file paths or streams.

keySignals.text: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName() uses the font manager to resolve fonts. So the moment you install your fonts on the system FromFamilyName should work
keySignals.interpretation: Partial answer: family name must match system-installed family name, not full/PostScript name.

codeInvestigation.finding: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName() accepts a family name string and calls sk_typeface_create_from_name. If no installed font matches the family name, Skia silently returns a fallback typeface rather than null. There is no overload that accepts a full font name.

SKFontManager.Default.FontFamilies enumerates all system-installed font family names (IEnumerable<string>). GetFontStyles(string familyName) returns an SKFontStyleSet containing all typefaces for that family.

SKFontStyleSet.GetStyleName(int index) returns the style name string (e.g., 'Regular', 'Bold', 'Heavy') for each typeface in the set. CreateTypeface(int index) materialises the typeface. This pair enables mapping full-name strings to typefaces without file access.

workarounds: Use SKTypeface.FromFamilyName('FrutigerNext LT', SKFontStyle.Normal) for regular — pass the family name only, not the full name
Enumerate SKFontManager.Default.FontFamilies and match by concatenating familyName + ' ' + GetStyleName(i) to find the typeface for a given full name
Load font from file or stream (SKTypeface.FromFile / SKTypeface.FromStream) and then read its .FamilyName property to discover the correct family name
nextQuestions: Are the FrutigerNext fonts installed on the target system, or do they need to be embedded/loaded from files?
What platform is the target — mobile platforms require fonts to be bundled, not just system-installed.

resolution.hypothesis: The user needs to separate the family name from the style suffix in the font full name, then pass only the family name to FromFamilyName() with an appropriate SKFontStyle — or enumerate font families to find a match.

proposals.title: Use family name with style parameter
proposals.description: Strip the style suffix from the full font name and pass the family name to FromFamilyName() with the appropriate SKFontStyle weight.

proposals.title: Resolve full font name to typeface by enumeration
proposals.description: Enumerate all installed font families and style names to find the typeface whose family+style matches the given full font name. Works without knowing the split point in advance.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Use family name with style parameter
resolution.recommendedReason: Simplest approach when the family name is known. Full font names follow a consistent 'FamilyName StyleName' pattern, so splitting the last word gives the style.

actionability.reason: Usage question about Skia's font model. API works as designed — it uses family names, not full/PostScript names. The answer is derivable from SKFontManager and SKFontStyleSet APIs. A contributor already partially answered in comments.

actions.description: Post answer explaining font family vs full name distinction with working code examples and platform caveats
Close as answered — usage question with documented API solution
